title: Crispy Smoky Sichuan Haddock Stir-fry
description: A fragrant, low‑carb Sichuan stir‑fry featuring smoky haddock and crisp air‑fried flakes, tossed with Sichuan peppercorns, chili, garlic and ginger for a zesty umami punch.
instructions: Set the air fryer to 200 °C (390 °F) and let it preheat for 3 minutes.
Pat the haddock fillets dry with paper towels; season lightly with salt and pepper.
Place the fillets in the air fryer basket in a single layer and air‑fry for 7 minutes until the edges are crisp and the fish flakes easily.
While the fish cooks, heat 1 tbsp of sesame oil in a wok or large skillet over medium‑high heat.
Add the Sichuan peppercorns and dried chilies; stir for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add minced garlic and ginger; sauté until aromatic, about 1 minute.
Add the broccoli florets (and any other low‑carb veggies you like) and stir‑fry for 3 minutes until bright green and slightly tender.
Return the cooked haddock to the pan; pour in soy sauce and Shaoxing wine, then toss everything together so the sauce coats the fish and veggies.
Let the mixture cook for another 2 minutes, allowing flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly.
Serve hot, garnished with sliced green onions and a sprinkle of sesame seeds if desired.
